comments.comment:
COVID-19 response and recovery aspects resulted in a reduced demand for electricity in New Brunswick during 2020. Operational requirements, market conditions and price influenced when fossil unit operation was required during the year. 

Gross generation for NB Power’s heavy fuel oil and petroleum coke-fired Coleson Cove Generating Station decreased 62.2 percent from 319,602 MWh in 2019 to 120,816 MWh in 2020 during the year as a result of a lower capacity factor for the station.

comments.comment:
COVID-19 response and recovery aspects resulted in a reduced demand for electricity in New Brunswick during 2020. Operational requirements, market conditions and price influenced when fossil unit operation was required during the year. 

While gross generation for NB Power’s heavy fuel oil and petroleum coke-fired Coleson Cove Generating Station decreased 62.2 percent from 319,602 MWh in 2019 to 120,816 MWh in 2020 during the year as a result of a lower capacity factor for the station, the facility commissioned a new auxiliary boil in February 2020. This boiler added to the annual sulphur dioxide emissions release from the station as the boiler was operated to provide plant heat during periods when Units 1, 2 and 3 were off line. During these periods emissions are directed to the stack while the flue gas desulphurization unit is not in service.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
Gross generation for Coleson Cove increased 416.6 percent from 120,816 MWh in 2020 to 503,302 MWhr in 2021 as a result of a higher capacity factor for the station.


Factors Contributing to Annual Emissions in 2021

NB Power continued its journey along the path towards a post-pandemic recovery. Gradual improvements in provincial conditions reflected economic conditions similar to pre-pandemic levels. Higher generation requirements, to meet increased sales volumes, were experienced during the year. Operational requirements, market conditions and price influenced when fossil unit operation was required during the year. Overall, fossil-fuel generation increased 31.7%. 

Emission rates vary due to several factors including fuel utilization and blend, boiler condition, operating load, and hours of operation. The availability of hydro and nuclear generation influences the capacity factor of fossil-fired units on an annual basis.

The percentage of in-province sales, for calendar year 2021, supplied by renewable and non-emitting resources was approximately 79%; above the five year average (2016-2020) of 75.6 %. In determining this percentage, NB Power includes generation and purchases; renewable and non-emitting includes NB Power’s own Hydro and Nuclear generation as well as purchases from renewable and non-emitting sources (hydro, wind, biomass and other embedded generation).

Renewal generation (wind and solar) is an intermittent resource, with varied and largely unpredictable generation. This variability creates an average daily ramping requirement. This highlights the challenges of balancing intermittent resources such as wind or solar. Additionally, any thermal units needed to meet the next day’s peak demand, balance load or balance renewables must remain online, as these units cannot be cycled on and off on a daily basis.

The emission intensity rates vary during operation of the fossil-fired generating stations. During times of electricity purchases and / or decreased energy demand, lower operating loads can result in less efficient generation from fossil-fired stations. This is a function of the physical nature of the boilers and the thermodynamics under which they operate.

Renewal generation (wind and solar) is an intermittent resource, with varied and largely unpredictable generation. This variability creates an average daily ramping requirement. This highlights the challenges of balancing intermittent resources such as wind or solar. Additionally, any thermal units needed to meet the next day’s peak demand, balance load or balance renewables must remain online, as these units cannot be cycled on and off on a daily basis.

The emission intensity rates vary during operation of the fossil-fired generating stations. During times of electricity purchases and / or decreased energy demand, lower operating loads can result in less efficient generation from fossil-fired stations. This is a function of the physical nature of the boilers and the thermodynamics under which they operate.

comments.comment: D'apres les declartions fournies par NB Power, seulement le Vanadium et le Nickel sont evacués dans l'atmosphère. La quantité de Plomb présente dans l’eau a été évalué à l'aide des analyses de laboratoires effectuées et fournies dans le fichier csv " cc_wte_data_2022".

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: La nouvelle méthode d'estimation prends en compte les données fournies dans le rapport "December 2022 Annual Coleson Cove Solid Waste Facility Report" en 2024. En effet le calcul de la quantité de Plomb éliminée sur place se fait à l'aide de la composition, de la quantité de boue et de la quantité sulfate de calcium  éliminées sur le site fournies dans le rapport. L'ancienne méthode d'estimation utilisait la des données d'années précédentes pour le calcul et n'etait donc plus répresentative de la situation de NB Power en 2022.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: De nouveaux rapports ont été fournis. Pour le calcul de la récupération de métaux et leurs composés, la quantité de "Botton Ash" vendue doit etre utilisée pour évaluer le Plomb récyclé. En effet dans le rapport "Fly Ash Bottom Ash 2022", la quantité de "Botton Ash" vendue déclarer est de zéro. Ce qui implique qu'il n'y a pas non plus de Plomb récupéré dans le "Botton Ash". Pour la récupération de résidus de dépollution, la quantité de sulfate de calcium récyclée  a été utilisé pour le calcul de la quantité de Plomb. Cette quantité provient du rapport "December 2022 Annual Coleson Cove Solid Waste Facility Report". L'ancienne méthode d'estimation utilisait la des données d'années précédentes pour le calcul et n'etait donc plus répresentative de la situation de NB Power en 2022.

comments.comment: La nouvelle méthode d’estimation prend en compte la quantité de particules totales pour le calcul de la quantité du Vanadium rejetée dans l'atmosphère. En effet la quantité de particules totales pour l'année 2022 a été fourni dans un rapport d'emission effectué en 2024 "Coleson Cove Emissions Report 2022". L'ancienne méthode d'estimation utilisait des facteurs d'émissions et non la composition et les quatités  réelles de la boue et du suftate de calcium en 2022. Ce calcul n'etait donc pas répresentatif de la situation de NB Power en 2022. La quantité de vanadium présente dans l’eau a été évalué à l'aide des analyses de laboratoires effectuées et fournies dans le fichier csv " cc_wte_data_2022".
